headlines.title: GOODRICH ROUTE BOOK FOR TEXAS RECEIVED

headlines.title: Demand for Logs Indicates Many Colorado Auto Tours

headlines.fullText: Heavy Colorado tourist traffic for the summer is indicated by the demand for Goodrich Automobile Books, completing those for this section The after interest in commeterjer from Fort Worth to Wiito trips from Texas to Colorado. headlines.title: FT. WORTH MEN ARE ELIGIBLE FOR CLUB
headlines.subtitle: Extension of Cadillac 50,000 Mile Organization Is Sought.
headlines.fullText: Application will be made for exten. ston of the Cadillac 50,000 Mile Clubs to Texas, Such a club has been forme4 In California. Members who have Cad- Illacs driven over 50.000 miles are en- titled to membership and they are fur- nished handsome radiator plates bear- Ing the Cadillac name and "50,000 Mile If such a club is established In Texas Fort Worth has at least four eligibles. M. Ellis has driven over the 50,000 n his Cadillac, which was the first four-cylinder car in Fort Worth ... B. Parks Is still driving a two-cylinder Cadillac of the tsge iss, 600 miles. C. R. Vickery also has a 1909 Cadillan entitle him to enrollment in the club. Cadillas used by the hand of engineers in all the Lake Worth res- ervoir work is another entitled to the Ervar Lova has driven it Emmo miles between Fort Worth and the reservoir, frequently in the worst weather, when rains threatened dam- age to some of the reservoir work. The trips were made, too, over rouds thae have been out Into deep gullies by the hauling of heavy machinery and ce- ment for a continuous period of mors than two years. Pmer to the work on the reservoir the car was used In the enitineering trips upon when all ane The car still is being used in the work and has had but one grinding. No other machine shop work has been done upon it.

headlines.title: This Auto Dealer in His First Car to Traverse U. S.
headlines.fullText: J. F. Tickle of Mission, Hidalgo county, is starting on a tour across the United States in a 40 horsepower Jack- son Olymple. Tickle had been consid- ering becoming a Jackson distributor when he took a trip to visit relatives In Van Wert, Ohio. While there, he made up his mind to do so, and, wiring James Liston here, closed the deal. He then went to Jackson, Mich., and got the car In which he will return. Westrand on his trip from El Paso to Attssion Is on the very southern bar- der of the United States, a short dis- tance from Brownsville, which monate that It Is the most southerly part of the country. Jackson is on lake shore, 80 Tickle will traverse the coun- try in returning to his home.

headlines.title: 'Trouble Hunter' for Jacksons to Make State Tour
headlines.fullText: T. N. Shambaugh, trouble hunter for the Jackson Automobile Company, will be in Fort Worth this week to start on a tour of Texas with James Liston Jr., state distributor for Jacksons. Shambaugh holds an unique position, He is an expert mechanic and is the man always consulted when the de- signers have done their best. He is expected to find weak places, If any, and acts much in the same capacity as "Pearson's Pessimist," the man em- ployed by the noted industrial develop- er to point out possibilities for fallure Shambaugh is being sent through the country as a part of the Jackson sery- ice system on the Imp, which will continue indefinitely, he will Consult and give them free service in adjust- Ing any difficulties they have have had with their cars through Inexpe- followed on the proposed trip, which will last als weeks or a month, has not been announced.
